recommendations: Probe localhost service availability with a single lsof check before issuing multiple curl attempts across different ports — units 20 and 27 each issued 3-5 speculative curl calls that all failed
Verify target file existence before Grep/Read on .env.local paths that may not exist in branch-specific worktrees — 3 units hit preventable ENOENT failures

Replace heredoc-based jq scripts written via Bash cat-redirect with Write tool directly — unit 8 took 3 attempts to land /tmp/aggregator.jq due to heredoc encoding issues that the Write tool would have avoided

Unit 13 produced zero output bytes for a 135-char input — investigate whether a tool call was dropped or the agent silently no-oped; zero-length responses without at least an acknowledgment indicate an underconfident non-response
Session recovery units (47) correctly re-read WAL and checkpoints before acting — this pattern is well-calibrated and should be retained

biases_detected: anchoring — repeated assumption that services (ports 3006/5006) and env files were present at expected paths before verifying; unit-20 made 5+ failed tool calls before confirming nothing was running

sunk_cost — unit-8 continued investing in the heredoc-based jq script approach (Write failed twice, Bash failed once) rather than pivoting to a simpler Write-then-Bash sequence earlier; 35+ tool calls for a task that resolved with a clean Write

confirmation_bias — unit-29 drilled into playwright trace binary after log grep confirmed a failure, but read .trace line-by-line rather than extracting the specific action frame that would confirm the root cause; gathered more evidence than needed to reach a conclusion already supported

scope_creep — unit-57 performed 5 WebSearch calls for a documentation task where existing repo files and prior session notes contained sufficient pattern data; external search added latency without materially changing the output
recommendations: pre-flight environment check before any multi-step workflow: one lsof/pm2/ls probe batch at the start of a unit rather than discovering missing state mid-execution across multiple failed calls
for shell scripts written to /tmp, prefer Write-then-Bash over heredoc-in-Bash; the heredoc path is fragile under the tool sandbox and caused the unit-8 retry spiral

cap trace/log deep-reads at the first frame that confirms the hypothesis; in unit-29 the root cause (onSuccess routing to error branch) was visible in the first 30 lines of the grep result but reading continued for 4 more tool calls

when context-restoring (unit-47 pattern), read WAL checkpoint first and only read additional files if the checkpoint is stale or ambiguous — unit-47 read 9 files sequentially where the checkpoint + git log would have been sufficient

complexity_underestimation: bc59cf34-8 jq aggregator took significantly more iterations than the approach complexity implied, suggesting underestimation of jq heredoc/encoding edge cases
recommendations: verify CWD and key env vars at unit start before issuing path-dependent Bash commands — a single pwd + ls check prevents the majority of observed failures

before assuming services are available, run pm2 list and lsof -iTCP:PORT -sTCP:LISTEN as a preflight rather than discovering failures mid-task

when monitoring background test runs, avoid sleep-based polling in Bash; use the TaskOutput or Monitor tool directly — the sleep 60 call in bc59cf34-24 failed and wasted a tool round-trip

Per dream insight 2026-06-18 (18+ recordings without mechanical gate = the missing gate is the root cause, not the lack of recording): further advisory recording is itself the thrash pattern. The only effective intervention is a PreToolUse hook that inspects the full Bash command string for ls(-la|-t|-d|-l)? [path], sed -n pattern, and cat [file] (non-heredoc) forms and returns a blocking additionalContext suggesting Read, LS, or Glob alternatives with the specific tool call to make. Until such a gate exists, the bias is structurally unaddressable.

ls-t-for-timestamp-sort subvariant is STABLE (five consecutive instances in session 4b1b8967 alone) and partially justified since LS and Glob have no sort-by-mtime mode. Recommend adding an approved shell alternative to rules/shell.md: python3 -c 'import glob,os; print(max(glob.glob(PATTERN), key=os.path.getmtime))' or fd PATTERN --type f | xargs stat -f '%m %N' | sort -rn | head -1 | cut -d' ' -f2-. Agents using ls -t are filling a real gap; give them the right tool rather than only prohibiting the wrong one.

sed-instead-of-Read-with-offset appearing in 5 units suggests agents are unaware Read accepts offset and limit parameters. Recommend adding a concrete example inline in the shell rules or CLAUDE.md: 'Read(file, offset=184, limit=16) replaces sed -n '185,200p' file'. The two primitives are functionally identical; the gap is documentation visibility.

grep_in_compound_bash persists because the prefer-ripgrep hook checks argv[0] only. Recommend extending the hook to scan the full Bash string for word-boundary grep matches not at position 0: rg '(;|&&|\|)\s*grep\b' <<< "$command" and emit a non-blocking warning. Full blocking is not appropriate since some grep -v filter patterns have no rg equivalent, but visibility would address the pattern.
